title: Nestotus lanuginosus
taxonomy: Nestotus lanuginosus is a species of flowering plant in the family Phrymaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The classification of this plant is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, class Magnoliopsida, order Lamiales, family Phrymaceae, genus Nestotus, and species N. lanuginosus. The family Phrymaceae is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as lopseed or monkey flowers. Nestotus lanuginosus is a unique species within this family due to its distinct characteristics and features.

intro: Nestotus lanuginosus, also known as Desert Marigold, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Boraginaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, skin irritations, and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Nestotus lanuginosus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its pharmacological activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Desert Marigold include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and skin conditions.
